classes.label: Conference
classes.description: Conference or event record derived from the conferences table.

classes.label: Organization
classes.description: Organization record derived from the organizations table.

classes.label: Paper
classes.description: Scholarly paper derived from the papers table.

classes.label: Person
classes.description: Person derived from the persons table.

classes.label: Topic
classes.description: Topic or subject category derived from the topics table.

classes.label: Paper Topic Relation
classes.description: Reified relation between a paper and a topic because rel_paper_topic contains an extra attribute RelationType.

assumptions: rel_person_paper is treated as a person-to-paper relation and labeled as authorship based on table naming and publication context, but this semantic is not explicitly enforced by the schema.
rel_person_topic is treated as a person-to-topic relation indicating interest or association; exact semantics are not explicit in the schema.
rel_paper_topic is modeled as a reified class because it contains the extra attribute RelationType.
omissions: papers.Publish was not mapped because no explicit foreign-key evidence or reliable semantic interpretation was available.
Type columns in persons and organizations were not converted into subclass axioms because values appear to be data-driven categories rather than stable schema-level entity types.
evidence_notes: Tool-derived join evidence strongly supports papers.Conference -> conferences.ConfID, organizations.Belongsto -> organizations.OrgID, and topics.ParentID -> topics.TopicID.

Connector tables rel_person_organization, rel_person_paper, rel_person_topic, and rel_paper_topic required partial inference for missing second-side joins based on column naming and primary-key patterns.
